message:
unify browser + Node: unscoped name, Node-aware default backend

- Renames npm package from @yourorg/pii-wasm to pii-wasm (unscoped).
- README rewrite reflects current state: three working backends, real
  measured numbers, browser + Node support, docs-site pointers.
- privacy-filter.ts default-backend logic now detects Node via
  process.versions.node (Node 21+ defines `navigator` globally so the
  old `typeof navigator === "undefined"` check was silently wrong).
  Node defaults to `backend:"wasm"` because ORT-Node's CPU EP has no
  kernel for this model's MatMulNBits / GatherBlockQuantized ops; tjs
  errors out at session create.

Verified:
- `await PrivacyFilter.create()` in Node: works, redacts correctly,
  forward 272 ms for a short input.
- `await PrivacyFilter.create({ backend: "wasm" })` in Node: same.
- Browser smoke + webgpu-smoke + webgpu-forward parity tests: still
  passing, 2/2 spans agreed vs tjs, argmax 32/32.

files.patch:
@@ -1,136 +1,134 @@
 # pii-wasm
 
-**Browser-native PII detection and redaction, based on `openai/privacy-filter`.**
+**PII detection and redaction for the browser and Node, powered by `openai/privacy-filter`.**
 
-Runs entirely on the user's device via WebAssembly (universal) or WebGPU (fast path on capable browsers). No network calls to external inference services. PII text never leaves the browser.
+Runs entirely on the user's device — WebGPU on capable browsers, WebAssembly + SIMD128 everywhere else. No backend, no network calls to inference services, no text ever leaves the device.
 
-## Why
+> The only npm package that runs `openai/privacy-filter` client-side with both GPU and CPU paths.
 
-OpenAI released `openai/privacy-filter` (April 20, 2026) — a 1.5B-parameter / 50M-active MoE token classifier for PII detection, Apache-2.0 licensed. It's marketed as browser-runnable; OpenAI's own Python SDK (`opf`) runs server-side.
+[**Docs**](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/) · [**Quickstart**](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/quickstart/) · [**API**](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/api/) · [**Benchmarks**](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/benchmarks/)
 
-The gap: no one has shipped a browser-native inference engine for it yet. That's what this repo is.
+## Install
 
-## Status
+```sh
+npm install pii-wasm
+```
 
-Scaffold — public API designed end-to-end, backend pending.
+## Use
 
-Roadmap tracked in `docs/roadmap.md`:
+```ts
+import { PrivacyFilter } from "pii-wasm";
 
-- Go/no-go measurement: MoE expert activation-frequency distribution (`scripts/measure-experts.py`).
-- Stage 0 backend: `transformers.js` baseline.
-- Stage 1 backend: Zig + WASM + SIMD128.
-- Stage 2 backend: WGSL.
-- Viterbi CRF decoder in JS (matches upstream calibration artifacts).
-- BIOES → span reconstruction.
-- Chunking for long inputs.
-- Browser demo + browser-extension wrapper.
+const filter = await PrivacyFilter.create();
 
-## Public API
+const result = await filter.redact(
+  "Hi, my name is John Smith and my email is john@example.com.",
+);
 
-```ts
-import { PrivacyFilter } from "@yourorg/pii-wasm";
-
-const filter = await PrivacyFilter.create({
-  onProgress: (p) => console.log(`${p.stage}: ${p.loaded}/${p.total}`),
-});
-
-const result = await filter.redact("Alice was born on 1990-01-02.");
-//  result.redactedText = "[private_person] was born on [private_date]."
-//  result.spans = [
-//    { label: "private_person", start: 0,  end: 5,  text: "Alice",      marker: "[private_person]" },
-//    { label: "private_date",   start: 18, end: 28, text: "1990-01-02", marker: "[private_date]"   },
-//  ]
-//  result.summary = { private_person: 1, private_date: 1 }
-```
+// result.redactedText
+//   "Hi, my name is [private_person] and my email is [private_email]."
 
-Custom markers, per-category enabling, batch input, and streaming are part of the design — see `src/js/types.ts`.
+// result.spans
+//   [ { label: "private_person", start: 15, end: 25, ... },
+//     { label: "private_email",  start: 43, end: 59, ... } ]
+```
 
-## Architecture
+Detection without applying redactions:
 
+```ts
+const { spans, containsPii } = await filter.detect(text);
 ```
-┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
-│  PrivacyFilter (public API)                             │
-│    create() / redact() / detect() / redactBatch()       │
-└────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────┘
-             │
-     ┌───────┴────────┐
-     │                │
-     ▼                ▼
-┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐
-│ Backend  │    │ Backend  │   WebGPU if available,
-│ WebGPU   │    │ WASM     │   WASM otherwise.
-└────┬─────┘    └────┬─────┘
-     │               │
-     └───────┬───────┘
-             │
-             ▼
-    ┌────────────────┐
-    │ Forward pass   │   Produces per-token logits
-    │ (weights from  │   over 33 classes (background + 8 spans × BIOES).
-    │  OPFS cache)   │
-    └────────┬───────┘
-             │
-             ▼
-    ┌────────────────────────┐
-    │ Viterbi CRF decoder    │   Uses loaded calibration.json
-    │ (JS-side)              │   to resolve transition biases.
-    └────────┬───────────────┘
-             │ best tag sequence
-             ▼
-    ┌────────────────────────┐
-    │ BIOES → span merger    │   Character-level spans.
-    └────────┬───────────────┘
-             │
-             ▼
-    ┌────────────────────────┐
-    │ Redaction applicator   │   Replace spans with markers.
-    └────────────────────────┘
-```
 
-The backend interface is the only thing that changes between WebGPU and WASM paths. Everything else (tokenization, Viterbi, span merging, redaction application) is shared JavaScript.
+Batch inputs, custom markers, per-category enabling — see the [API reference](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/api/).
+
+## Why
+
+OpenAI released [`openai/privacy-filter`](https://huggingface.co/openai/privacy-filter) on 2026-04-20 — a 1.5B-parameter MoE (50M active) bidirectional token classifier for PII detection. Apache 2.0. State-of-the-art on PII-Masking-300k (96% F1).
 
-## Research plan
+The official SDK is Python. `transformers.js` runs it on WebGPU but fails on CPU — ORT-Web's WASM bundle is missing `GatherBlockQuantized` and `MatMulNBits` kernels. **pii-wasm is the only package that runs this model end-to-end in the browser on both GPU and CPU**, with a single public API.
 
-Three-stage cost reduction, each independently validatable:
+## Performance
 
-**Stage 0 — `transformers.js` baseline.**
-Working end-to-end browser inference. Benchmark memory + latency. Establishes the number to beat.
+M-series MacBook / Chromium 147, median of 5 runs after 2 warmups. Full table in [/benchmarks](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/benchmarks/):
 
-**Stage 1 — Zig + WASM + SIMD128 custom inference.**
-Hand-rolled int4 MoE matmul + attention + router. Target: ~2× faster than transformers.js WASM backend; 30-50% lower memory; universal browser support.
+| | tjs (WebGPU) | **pii-wasm (WebGPU)** | speedup |
+|---|---:|---:|---:|
+| Short input | 29 ms | **7 ms** | **4.1×** |
+| Medium input | 39 ms | **12 ms** | **3.2×** |
+| Long input | 57 ms | **25 ms** | **2.3×** |
+| Second-visit warmup | 11.2 s | **1.1 s** | **10×** |
 
-**Stage 2 — WebGPU path with hand-tuned WGSL.**
-Custom WGSL matmul + MoE dispatch. Target: ~2× faster than transformers.js WebGPU backend; preferred when `navigator.gpu` is available.
+Cold-start parity on first visit (both download ~770 MB from HF CDN); repeat visits hit our OPFS cache.
 
-**Stage 3 — MoE-specific compression (novel).**
+## Backends
 
-MoE models have structural redundancy a dense model doesn't: 384 experts per layer, many rarely activated. Stackable techniques:
+Three interchangeable engines, same public API:
 
-| Technique | Expected shrink | Notes |
+| Backend | Path | When |
 |---|---|---|
-| Int4 baseline quantization | 4× | Standard, minimal accuracy loss |
-| Expert-cluster codebook (K-means over experts) | 3-5× on experts | Novel; repurposes PLE-codebook pattern for MoE |
-| Cold-expert pruning (drop low-activation experts) | 1.5-2× | Wanda-style, MoE-aware |
-| Hadamard rotation + int3 residual | 1.3× | QuIP#-style incoherence processing |
+| `auto` *(default)* | transformers.js, WebGPU via ORT-Web | Drop-in, works everywhere tjs works |
+| `webgpu` | Hand-tuned WGSL compute shaders | **Fastest.** Modern Chromium / Safari / Firefox with WebGPU + `shader-f16` |
+| `wasm` | Zig + SIMD128, compiled to WebAssembly | Universal fallback. Only working CPU path for this model |
 
-Stacked: theoretical ~20-50× reduction vs fp16. Target ~150 MB on disk → fits Cloudflare Workers (128 MB memory cap).
+```ts
+const gpu = await PrivacyFilter.create({ backend: "webgpu" });   // fastest
+const wasm = await PrivacyFilter.create({ backend: "wasm" });    // universal
+const auto = await PrivacyFilter.create({ backend: "auto" });    // tjs baseline
+```
+
+All three produce byte-identical spans on the same input. See [/backends](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/backends/).
 
-**Go/no-go for Stage 3:** `scripts/measure-experts.py` measures per-expert activation frequency on the calibration corpus. Goal: confirm long-tail distribution that justifies cluster + prune. ~2 hours of Python; binary signal.
+## Architecture
 
-## Non-goals
+```
+ ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
+ │ PrivacyFilter (public API)          │
+ │ create / redact / detect / dispose  │
+ └────────────┬────────────────────────┘
+              │
+              ▼
+ ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
+ │ Tokenizer → Chunking → Backend      │
+ │ → Viterbi CRF → BIOES merge         │
+ │ → Redaction applicator              │
+ └────────────┬────────────────────────┘
+              │
+              ▼
+   ┌──────┬──────┬──────┐
+   │ tjs  │ wgpu │ wasm │  backends (interchangeable)
+   └──────┴──────┴──────┘
+              │
+              ▼
+      ONNX weights (770 MB)
+      ↑ cached in OPFS ↑
+```
 
-- **Not** a wrapper around the OpenAI Python CLI.
-- **Not** an HTTP API to a remote inference service.
-- **Not** a multi-tenant server product.
-- **Not** a fine-tuning tool.
+Full architecture details in [/architecture](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/architecture/).
 
-This library does ONE thing: run privacy-filter inference in the browser, as efficiently as possible, with a clean API.
+## Caveats
 
-## License
+`openai/privacy-filter` is a detection aid, **not an anonymization guarantee**. No dedicated SSN / passport label. English-first (Japanese ~88% F1, other languages untested). Short text under-contextualizes.
 
-Apache 2.0 — matches upstream.
+See the [caveats page](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/caveats/) and OpenAI's [model card](https://cdn.openai.com/pdf/c66281ed-b638-456a-8ce1-97e9f5264a90/OpenAI-Privacy-Filter-Model-Card.pdf) before treating redacted output as compliance-safe.
 
-## Upstream
+## Development
+
+```sh
+npm install
+npm run build           # Zig → WASM, JS bundle, .d.ts
+npm run typecheck
+npm run test            # playwright browser tests
+```
+
+Source layout:
+
+- `src/js/` — public API, backends, inference composition (Viterbi, chunking, redaction).
+- `src/zig/` — Zig kernels (int4 matmul, banded attention, RoPE, QMoE dispatch, …) compiled to WASM.
+- `src/js/backends/webgpu.ts` — WGSL shaders for the Stage-2 GPU backend.
+- `docs/` — engineering notes (roadmap, benchmarks, measurement artefacts).
+- `docs-site/` — Astro + Starlight user docs, deployed to GitHub Pages.
+- `tests/browser/` — Playwright parity + benchmark tests.
+
+## License
 
-- Model: https://huggingface.co/openai/privacy-filter
-- Reference implementation: https://github.com/openai/privacy-filter
+Apache 2.0, matching the upstream model.
